confidence_tiers.CONFIRMED: Directly supported by a cited primary or secondary source
confidence_tiers.POSSIBLE: Plausible from one source or logical inference only
confidence_tiers.UNVERIFIED: Noted but not corroborated by any source

birth.year_notes: Beaumont HS 1952 state champion → age 17-18 → birth ~1934-1936; Spokeo 'age 92' in 2026 implies birth c. 1933-1934
birth.exact_date_notes: Not found in any accessible free public source. Required: Texas Birth Certificate (~$22) or FamilySearch Texas Birth Index (requires browser login).

current_status.notes: Spokeo shows 'Dale W. Spence, Age 92, Houston TX' with Hunter Spence listed as relative. Rice faculty/emeritus directory listing confirmed as of Wayback snapshot December 2025. No death record, obituary, or Find a Grave memorial found.

father.name_notes: Not found in any source. Most likely identified via: Texas Birth Certificate for Dale Sr. (names parents); 1940 US Census Jefferson County TX; LSU dissertation vita (currently inaccessible).
father.birthplace_region: NE England — Yorkshire (North Riding) or County Durham
father.birthplace_notes: Spence surname distribution: highest English concentration in NE England. First English documentary record is Yorkshire 1300 (Guisborough Priory). County Durham documented 1910 (Tristram F Spence). No specific record of Dale Sr.'s father's birthplace found.
father.immigration_motivation: Oil/refinery labor — Spindletop 1901 triggered British technical migration to Beaumont/Port Arthur corridor

mother.maiden_name_hypothesis: Byrd (family tradition — John Archie Asner Byrd 1868 + Martha Alice Bedford)
mother.maiden_name_notes: No record corroborates 'Dovie Byrd' as a named individual. Byrd is a documented Southern US surname consistent with SE Texas demographics. Search required: Texas Marriage Index 1837-1973 for 'Dovie Byrd' + Spence.
mother.nationality_notes: Behind the Name classifies 'Dovie' as American (South) female given name with no English or Scottish parallel. Confirms mother was American-born.

spouse_death.place: Beaumont, Jefferson County, Texas (assumed — buried there)

spouse_burial.cemetery: Forest Lawn Memorial Park and Funeral Home

marriage.marriage_location: Beaumont, TX (possible — both families anchored there by 1950s)

children.name: Dale William Spence Jr.
children.notes: Family interview only; not independently verified

siblings.notes: No siblings of Dale Sr. named in any accessible source. Required: 1940 Census Jefferson County TX (lists all children in household) or obituary for Dale Sr.

education.institution: North Texas State University (now University of North Texas)

education.dissertation_title: Analysis of Selected Values in Physical Education as Identified by Professional Personnel
education.notes: Full text (vita section) inaccessible via Wayback — only front matter (6 pages) captured. Would name parents and birth date.

education.institution: Baylor University / Baylor College of Medicine

high_school.championship: Texas state high school championship, 880-yard run

notable.bill_cosby_connection: Ran track with Bill Cosby in the late 1950s (Cosby attended Temple University c. 1959-1964)

athletics.yearbook_ocr_note: 1952 Pine Burr yearbook OCR text reads '440 dash' — this is an OCR error. All other sources confirm 880-yard run.

department_names.1990: Department of Human Performance and Health Sciences
department_names.note: Same unit; renamed during his tenure

fellowship.name: Fellow, American College of Sports Medicine (FACSM)

nasa_connection.description: Summer fellowships in Aeronautics and Space Research, both during and following the Apollo 11 space mission

publications.affiliation: Department of Human Performance and Health Sciences, Rice University, Houston TX 77251-1892

timeline.event: Won Texas state HS championship, 880-yard run, Beaumont High School
Undergraduate student, Rice University, Houston TX
Graduated Rice; commissioned as Marine Corps officer
Served in US Marine Corps Reserve; retired as Colonel (35 years)
EdD, Louisiana State University — dissertation on values in physical education
NASA summer fellowships in Aeronautics and Space Research during and following Apollo 11 mission
Postdoctoral fellowship, Baylor College of Medicine (Anatomy, Physiology, Medicine depts.)
Published research (PubMed PMID 2375667) — Dept. Human Performance and Health Sciences, Rice
Rice News reference: Professor of Kinesiology Dale Spence, ran track with Bill Cosby
Retired from Rice after 40 years on faculty
Wife Alice Marie Henslee Spence died, age 69, Beaumont TX
Named in Rice Athletics PR Club honor; gave Veterans Day remarks as Professor Emeritus of Kinesiology
Still listed in Rice faculty/emeritus directory (last Wayback snapshot)
